dataSourceNote: The structured data pipeline (Yahoo Finance, Google News, SEC EDGAR) was unreachable again this session (policy-level 403s at the proxy). Web search was used for qualitative price/news context instead of scripts/fetch-market-data.mjs + fetch-news.mjs + fetch-fundamentals.mjs — real but less precise than the structured OHLCV/technical-indicator/EDGAR feed. No new BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER was sized off this data; the existing AAPL position (opened 2026-07-14) was evaluated only against its own already-approved stop ($308) / take-profit ($338) plan.

webSearchFindings.AAPL: Closed $327.50 on 2026-07-15 (+4%, fresh record high), +20% YTD. Catalysts: China regulatory approval for Apple Intelligence with Alibaba's Qwen model powering it in China; strong iPhone supply-chain production signals; Broadcom chip deal through 2031. Q3 FY2026 earnings due 2026-07-30 (consensus EPS $1.88, +20% YoY). Sources: gurufocus.com, tradingkey.com, 247wallst.com, benzinga.com (via WebSearch).
webSearchFindings.MSFT: Closed $395.63 on 2026-07-15 (+3.19%), recovering from a 2026-07-14 selloff triggered by an IBM earnings warning that spooked enterprise-software names broadly (MSFT, ServiceNow, Salesforce, Intuit all affected). FY2026 Q4 earnings due 2026-07-29. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages (vantagemarkets.com, tradingkey.com).
webSearchFindings.NVDA: ~$211.81 on 2026-07-15 (+0.3%), muted vs. the broader tech rally despite strong fundamentals: reported 85% revenue growth to $81.6B, Data Center revenue +92% to $75.2B, $80B share buyback authorized, Vera Rubin AI accelerator confirmed in production. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages (fxleaders.com).
webSearchFindings.AMZN: $254.96 on 2026-07-15 (+3.0%), driven by strong early Prime Day spending reports. Flagged as ~8.5% above one source's fair-value estimate (overvalued per that source) despite a high (94/100) fundamentals score. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages (gurufocus.com, tradingkey.com).
webSearchFindings.GOOGL: ~$368.88 on 2026-07-15 (up from $359.51 on 07-14, +1.99% that day). Headline risks: EU reportedly preparing a Big Tech antitrust fine; a DeepMind researcher resignation over an AI-military-deal controversy. Q1 2026 context: revenue +22% to $109.9B, Cloud +63% to $20.0B. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages.
webSearchFindings.META: $681.31 on 2026-07-15 (+3.1%), +17% in July alone (third-best S&P 500 performer this month) after a sharp June-to-July sentiment reversal on its AI/data-center buildout (Hyperion, Louisiana) now shipping visible products. Regulatory headwinds: Brazil suspended a Meta privacy-policy update for AI training; EU formal DMA charges over its 'pay or consent' ad model. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages (benzinga.com, bloomberg.com).
webSearchFindings.TSLA: Closed $396.18 on 2026-07-15, trading ~$394-406 range on 2026-07-16. Mixed analyst moves: Morgan Stanley +$417 (from $415), Barclays +$370 (from $360), Wells Fargo (options-related) +$130 (from $125). Earnings due 2026-07-22 (~5.7% expected move). Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages.
webSearchFindings.AVGO: Closed $394.28 on 2026-07-15 (+1.32%), ~$393 on 2026-07-16. Apple chip-supply deal through 2031 reaffirmed (~$30B+). CEO Hock Tan: AI XPU/networking demand 'insatiable', 2027 AI revenue to 'very easily' exceed $100B. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages.
webSearchFindings.JPM: Closed $346.91 on 2026-07-15 (+1.17%), record Q2 earnings ($16.9B adjusted net income, $57.34B revenue, +27% YoY), multiple analyst price-target hikes (Wells Fargo to $375, RBC to $370), nearing a $1T market cap. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages (benzinga.com, themarketsdaily.com).
webSearchFindings.SPY/QQQ: S&P 500 +0.38% to 7,543.59 on 2026-07-15; QQQ +0.64% premarket to $724.48. Driven by cooler June CPI (3.5% YoY vs 3.8% expected), easing Fed rate-hike expectations, plus a strong start to earnings season (e.g. Morgan Stanley beat). Source: WebSearch aggregated finance/news pages (Motley Fool, Benzinga).
webSearchFindings.XLE: $55.99 on 2026-07-15, down from a $56.95 prior close, pulling back on a reported US-Iran peace deal easing the oil supply-crunch narrative that had been driving energy-sector strength. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance pages.

AAPL.status: Marked around ~$327.50 per web search (2026-07-15 close) — solid unrealized gain, still between stop and target, held unchanged.

decisionProcess: Applied the system prompt v2's 7-role deliberation using the qualitative web-search findings above. 2026-07-15 was a broad, single-catalyst (cooler CPI / rate-cut hopes) rally across nearly every mega-cap watchlist name (AAPL, MSFT, AMZN, META, JPM all +3% or more) — a correlation/concentration red flag under the Risk Manager's own criteria, since 'independent' candidates today would really be one macro bet repeated several times. Combined with this session's degraded (web-search-only) data precision, every new-position candidate was vetoed at the Risk Manager step. The existing AAPL long remains within its original stop/target band and continues unchanged.

entries._dataAsOf: 2026-07-15 (web search fallback — structured pipeline unreachable this session, see dataSourceNote)
